title.zh: 大模型部署与微调 — 理论学习

note.zh: 正在学习大模型部署和微调的理论知识——了解 LoRA 原理、推理优化、模型量化等概念。目前是理论学习阶段，还没有在本地跑过微调实验（设备配置有限），但理解原理本身就是重要的第一步。
note.en: Studying the theory behind LLM deployment and fine-tuning — LoRA principles, inference optimization, model quantization. Currently at the theory stage; havent run fine-tuning experiments locally yet (limited hardware), but understanding the principles is itself an important first step.

keyPoints.zh: LoRA 通过低秩矩阵分解，只需训练极少参数即可适配下游任务
keyPoints.en: LoRA uses low-rank matrix decomposition to adapt models with very few trainable parameters

keyPoints.zh: Ollama 让本地运行大模型变得极其简单，一行命令即可
keyPoints.en: Ollama makes running LLMs locally trivially easy — just one command

keyPoints.zh: 模型量化（INT8/INT4）大幅降低显存占用，几乎不影响效果
keyPoints.en: Model quantization (INT8/INT4) drastically reduces memory usage with minimal quality loss

keyPoints.zh: DeepSeek 的 MoE 架构让大模型推理成本大幅下降
keyPoints.en: DeepSeek's MoE architecture significantly reduces LLM inference costs

content.zh: 我报名了大模型部署与微调的课程，目前正在学习理论知识。由于设备配置有限（参数量大的模型跑不动），还未进行实际微调操作，但理解这些概念本身就是为未来打基础。
content.en: I enrolled in an LLM deployment and fine-tuning course, currently studying the theory. I havent done actual fine-tuning yet due to limited hardware, but understanding these concepts is building a foundation for the future.

content.zh: 课程内容概览
content.en: Course Overview

items.zh: 了解开源模型的本地部署流程
items.en: Understanding the workflow of local model deployment

items.zh: 学习 LoRA/QLoRA 参数高效微调的原理
items.en: Learning the principles of parameter-efficient fine-tuning (LoRA/QLoRA)

items.zh: 推理优化的概念：量化、KV Cache、vLLM
items.en: Concepts of inference optimization: quantization, KV cache, vLLM

content.zh: 微调不是从零训练，而是在预训练模型的基础上，用少量标注数据让模型适应特定任务。
content.en: Fine-tuning is not training from scratch — it adapts a pre-trained model to specific tasks with small labeled datasets.

content.zh: 我的收获
content.en: My Takeaways

items.zh: 理解了 LoRA 的原理：通过低秩矩阵分解来近似权重更新，大幅降低微调门槛
items.en: Understood LoRA: low-rank matrix decomposition approximates weight updates, dramatically lowering the fine-tuning barrier

items.zh: 了解了 Ollama 本地运行模型的流程
items.en: Learned about the workflow for running models locally with Ollama

items.zh: 明白了模型量化的作用：降低显存占用，让普通设备也能跑大模型
items.en: Understood quantization: reducing memory usage so larger models can run on consumer hardware

items.zh: 对 MoE 架构（如 DeepSeek）的设计思路有了概念性理解
items.en: Gained conceptual understanding of MoE architecture design (e.g., DeepSeek)

content.zh: 下一步计划：在自己机器上跑通一个完整的微调实验。
content.en: Next step: Run a complete fine-tuning experiment on my own machine.

title.zh: 关注 AI 前沿动态

note.zh: 持续关注 DeepSeek、OpenAI 等最新进展。开源模型的快速迭代让我对 AI 的未来充满期待，也促使我动手尝试。
note.en: Keeping up with the latest from DeepSeek, OpenAI, and others. The rapid iteration of open-source models excites me about the future and motivates me to build things myself.

keyPoints.zh: 开源模型（DeepSeek V4、Llama 4）正在快速缩小与闭源模型的差距
keyPoints.en: Open-source models (DeepSeek V4, Llama 4) are rapidly closing the gap with proprietary ones

keyPoints.zh: 多模态和 Agent 是 2026 年最重要的两个方向
keyPoints.en: Multimodal and Agent are the two most important trends of 2026

keyPoints.zh: 推理成本的下降让个人开发者也能用上顶级模型
keyPoints.en: Falling inference costs let individual developers access top-tier models

content.zh: 每天花 30 分钟浏览 AI 新闻已经成了我的习惯。以下是我对近期动态的观察和思考。
content.en: Spending 30 minutes on AI news has become a daily habit. Here are my observations and thoughts on recent developments.

content.zh: 开源 vs 闭源：差距正在缩小
content.en: Open vs Closed: The Gap is Closing

content.zh: DeepSeek V4 的表现令人惊叹——在多项基准上与 GPT-5 不相上下，而训练成本仅为闭源模型的几分之一。Llama 4 也在推理能力上大幅提升。开源模型的迭代速度已经超过了大多数人的预期。
content.en: DeepSeek V4 is stunning — matching GPT-5 on multiple benchmarks while costing a fraction to train. Llama 4 also shows major leaps in reasoning. The iteration speed of open-source models has exceeded most people's expectations.

content.zh: Agent 时代即将到来
content.en: The Agent Era is Coming

content.zh: 2026 年最热的词是「Agent」。从 Claude Code 到各种自动化 Agent 框架，AI 正在从「回答问题」进化到「完成任务」。这对我作为大一学生来说既是机遇也是挑战——学会用 AI 工具本身就是在为未来做准备。
content.en: The hottest word of 2026 is 'Agent.' From Claude Code to various automation frameworks, AI is evolving from 'answering questions' to 'completing tasks.' As a freshman, this is both opportunity and challenge — learning to use AI tools is itself preparation for the future.

content.zh: 我的感受
content.en: My Take

content.zh: 作为刚入门的 AI 学生，最让我兴奋的是进入门槛在持续降低。不需要 PhD 也能跑模型、做实验、构建应用。但这也意味着需要更自律——工具变强了，思考不能变弱。
content.en: As a beginner AI student, what excites me most is how the barrier to entry keeps dropping. No PhD needed to run models, do experiments, build applications. But this also means more self-discipline is needed — tools got stronger, thinking shouldn't get weaker.

title.zh: 我的书架：最近在读什么

note.zh: 除了 AI 和技术书，我也读哲学、心理学。涉猎广不是分心，是把世界看得更清楚。
note.en: Beyond AI and tech, I also read philosophy and psychology. Wide-ranging reading isn't distraction — it's how you see the world more clearly.

keyPoints.zh: 《白话机器学习的数学》——用通俗语言讲 ML 数学基础，适合入门
keyPoints.en: 'Math for ML (in plain language)' — ML math fundamentals explained simply, great for beginners

keyPoints.zh: 《被讨厌的勇气》——阿德勒心理学，学会不被他人评价左右
keyPoints.en: 'The Courage to Be Disliked' — Adlerian psychology, learning to not be controlled by others' judgment

keyPoints.zh: 《普林斯顿微积分读本》——从高中开始读，微积分入门经典
keyPoints.en: 'The Calculus Lifesaver' — started in high school, classic calculus入门

content.zh: 读书对我来说不是"打卡"，而是在好奇心驱动下自然地探索。以下是最近在读和读过的一些书。
content.en: Reading for me isn't about checking boxes — it's exploring naturally, driven by curiosity. Here are some books I've been reading.

content.zh: AI 与数学
content.en: AI & Math

items.zh: 《白话机器学习的数学》——用最直白的语言讲回归、分类、正则化背后的数学
items.en: 'Math for ML in Plain Language' — regression, classification, regularization math explained in plain terms

items.zh: 《普林斯顿微积分读本》——高中开始啃的微积分入门书，断断续续在读
items.en: 'The Calculus Lifesaver' — started in high school, a friendly intro to calculus I keep coming back to

content.zh: 人文与社科
content.en: Humanities & Social Science

items.zh: 《毛泽东选集》——理解中国近现代史和思维方式的重要窗口
items.en: 'Selected Works of Mao Zedong' — important lens for understanding modern Chinese history and thinking

items.zh: 《传习录》——王阳明心学经典，"知行合一"对我影响很大
items.en: 'Instructions for Practical Living' — Wang Yangming's classic on the unity of knowledge and action

content.zh: 心理学
content.en: Psychology

items.zh: 《非暴力沟通》——学会不带评判地表达，对人际关系帮助很大
items.en: 'Nonviolent Communication' — learning to express without judgment, hugely helpful for relationships

items.zh: 《自卑与超越》——阿德勒谈自卑感的来源和超越之道
items.en: 'What Life Should Mean to You' — Adler on the sources of inferiority and paths to overcome it

items.zh: 《被讨厌的勇气》——阿德勒心理学通俗版，不被他人认可也可以自由
items.en: 'The Courage to Be Disliked' — Adlerian psychology for the modern reader, freedom from needing others' approval

content.zh: 涉猎广泛不是分心，是把世界看得更清楚。——这也是我为人大一的真实写照。
content.en: Wide-ranging reading isn't distraction — it's how you see the world more clearly. This is my honest freshman-year self.

title.zh: Vibe Coding 初体验：用 AI 写代码是什么感觉

note.zh: 从 Copilot 换到 Claude Code，最大的感受不是代码变快了，而是思考方式变了——先想清楚要什么，再让 AI 帮你实现。这和踢球一样：先阅读场上形势，再做动作。
note.en: Switching from Copilot to Claude Code — the biggest change isn't speed, it's mindset. Think clearly about what you want, then let AI help you build it. Just like football: read the field first, then move.

keyPoints.zh: Vibe Coding 的核心不是"AI 替你写代码"，而是"先想清楚要什么，再描述给 AI"
keyPoints.en: Vibe Coding's core isn't 'AI writes code for you' — it's 'think clearly about what you want, then describe it to AI'

keyPoints.zh: 工具从 Copilot 换到 Claude Code 后，开发效率提升了数倍
keyPoints.en: Switching from Copilot to Claude Code boosted development efficiency several times over

keyPoints.zh: AI 编程最大的改变是思维方式——从"怎么实现"变成"要做什么"
keyPoints.en: The biggest change is mindset — from 'how do I implement this' to 'what do I want to achieve'

content.zh: 从 Copilot 的代码补全到 Claude Code 的对话式开发，我的编程方式发生了根本性变化。这篇文章分享我的真实体验。
content.en: From Copilot code completion to Claude Code conversational development, my approach to programming has fundamentally changed. Here is my honest experience.

content.zh: 起点：Copilot 的代码补全
content.en: Starting Point: Copilot Code Completion

content.zh: 最开始用 Copilot 时，感觉就像有个聪明的自动补全助手。它能猜出我想写什么，省了不少打字时间。但本质上，还是我在写代码，AI 只是辅助。
content.en: When I first used Copilot, it felt like having a smart autocomplete assistant. It could guess what I wanted to write, saving typing time. But essentially, I was still writing code — AI was just helping.

content.zh: 转折：Claude Code 的对话式开发
content.en: Turning Point: Claude Code Conversational Dev

content.zh: Claude Code 完全改变了游戏规则。不是补全代码，而是理解需求——我描述想要什么，它生成完整方案。这种感觉不像在编程，更像在和一个有经验的工程师讨论。
content.en: Claude Code changed the game entirely. Not code completion, but understanding requirements — I describe what I want, it generates a complete solution. This feels less like programming and more like discussing with an experienced engineer.

content.zh: 这和踢球一样：先阅读场上形势，再做动作。不用每脚球都自己带——站好位置，传出去，效率更高。
content.en: Just like football: read the field first, then move. You don't need to dribble every ball — hold your position, pass, and you'll be more effective.

content.zh: 学会了什么
content.en: What I Learned

items.zh: 表述需求比写代码更重要——清晰的描述 > 精湛的技艺
items.en: Describing requirements matters more than writing code — clear description > exquisite skill

items.zh: AI 会犯错，需要你来判断——保持批判性思维
items.en: AI makes mistakes, you need to judge — keep critical thinking

items.zh: 小批量、高频次的开发节奏最适合 AI 协作
items.en: Small batch, high frequency development rhythm works best with AI

items.zh: 这个个人主页就是用 Vibe Coding 方式搭建的
items.en: This portfolio website was built entirely through Vibe Coding

title.zh: 从文科到 AI：一个交叉学科的视角
title.en: From Humanities to AI: A Cross-Disciplinary View

note.zh: 我对文史哲政经的兴趣和 AI 并不矛盾。AI 正在改变每一个领域——理解人、理解社会，才能做出真正有用的 AI。涉猎广泛不是分心，是积累。
note.en: My interest in humanities and AI aren't contradictory. AI is transforming every field — understanding people and society is key to building truly useful AI. Wide-ranging interests aren't distraction, they're investment.

keyPoints.zh: 理解人、理解社会，才能做出真正有用的 AI
keyPoints.en: Understanding people and society is key to building truly useful AI

keyPoints.zh: 涉猎广泛不是分心，是积累——不同领域的视角会在意想不到的地方交汇
keyPoints.en: Wide-ranging interests aren't distraction, they're investment — perspectives from different fields converge in unexpected places

keyPoints.zh: AI 的本质是工具，工具的价值取决于用工具的人
keyPoints.en: AI's essence is a tool, and a tool's value depends on who wields it

content.zh: 有人问我：你学 AI，为什么还花时间看文史哲政经？这不是分心吗？这篇文章是我的回答。
content.en: People ask me: you're studying AI, why spend time on humanities, history, politics, and economics? Isn't that distraction? Here's my answer.

content.zh: AI 不是孤立的技术
content.en: AI Is Not an Isolated Technology

content.zh: AI 正在进入每一个领域——法律、医疗、教育、金融、艺术。如果只懂技术不懂领域，就做不出真正有价值的东西。理解人和社会，才知道 AI 应该解决什么问题。
content.en: AI is entering every field — law, medicine, education, finance, art. If you only understand tech without understanding the domain, you cannot build truly valuable things. Understanding people and society tells you what problems AI should solve.

content.zh: 交叉学科的优势
content.en: The Cross-Disciplinary Advantage

content.zh: 读历史让我理解长期趋势，哲学让我思考智能的本质，政治经济让我看到技术背后的社会力量。这些看起来和代码无关的知识，实际上在帮我建立判断力——什么值得做，什么只是炒作。
content.en: History helps me understand long-term trends, philosophy makes me think about the nature of intelligence, politics and economics show me the social forces behind technology. This knowledge that seems unrelated to code actually helps me build judgment — what's worth doing vs. what's just hype.

content.zh: 最好的工程师不是最会写代码的人，而是最理解问题的人。
content.en: The best engineers aren't the ones who write the most code — they're the ones who understand problems best.
